mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, triggering a positive earnings revision cycle that lifts S&P 500 prices within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and household credit balances signals synchronized supply-demand recovery, compressing risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained manufacturing capacity, any sustained rise in the industrial production index signals pricing power expansion for corporations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises, or if the signal accuracy falls below 45% during supply bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising new housing starts consistently lead equity market appreciation over short horizons because construction activity directly reflects consumer confidence and credit availability, lifting S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution shows no positive skew when HOUST increases month-over-month, or the predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) against a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and real GDP expansion confirms a self-reinforcing economic upswing, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction effect reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI remains below 2.0 percent, positive revisions to retail sales data directly boost corporate margin expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases when CPI is below 2.0%, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels during stagflationary periods.
